b: AAA-style, player-facing photo mode for Unity: pause the game, fly a free camera, grade the image, apply filter presets and capture supersampled screenshots. The UI is reskinnable and gamepad-ready. Unity 2021.3 LTS or newer · URP, HDRP and the Built-in Render Pipeline.

One-component integration : drop the configured prefab into a scene (or use the Setup Wizard's Add Photo Mode To Scene button) and press your toggle key. Free camera with fly and orbit modes, sprint, roll, FOV zoom, optional collision and a configurable wander radius. Image adjustments in natural units : exposure (EV), contrast, saturation, temperature, vignette, film grain and bokeh depth of…
Quick Start : the 2-minute wizard path and the manual path. Core Concepts : how the controller, backends and UI fit together. Troubleshooting : if something looks wrong.

In the editor: Tools → Photo Mode Pro → Report a Bug (pre-filled email), or write to support.dan.max@gmail.com . Tools → Photo Mode Pro → Run Self-Test writes a PhotoModeSelfTest.json you can attach to any report.
Two ways in: the wizard path (about 2 minutes) or the manual path (about 10).

Unity 2021.3 LTS or newer (verified on 2021.3, 2022.3 and 6000.3). Any render pipeline: URP (12+), HDRP (12+) or Built-in . On Built-in, install com.unity.postprocessing (Post Processing Stack v2) for the full adjustment set; without it a minimal fallback backend is used. The right backend is detected automatically at runtime (override via PhotoModeSettings.backendOverride ). Optional: the Input…

Import Photo Mode Pro. A welcome window appears on first import (once per machine), or open Tools → Photo Mode Pro → Setup Wizard any time. The wizard checks your render pipeline, effects backend, input handling, optional packages, TextMesh Pro essentials and the demo materials, each with a green / yellow / red light, a one-line explanation and a Fix button where a fix can be automated (importing…

Drag Assets/PhotoModePro/Demo/Generated/PhotoModePro.prefab into your scene (menu equivalent: Tools → Photo Mode Pro → Add Photo Mode To Scene ). The prefab already references the default PhotoModeSettings , the UI prefab and the six built-in presets. Done. Press your toggle key in Play mode. Recommended wiring on the PhotoModeController component: Game Ui Canvases : add your HUD canvases so…

Open Assets/PhotoModePro/Demo/PhotoModeDemo.unity : a forest clearing at golden hour with a campfire, wandering animals and a playable adventurer (all bundled third-party assets are CC0; see Demo/ThirdParty/ATTRIBUTIONS.md ). Press Play. Move with WASD (or left stick), orbit with the mouse (or right stick), Shift to run, Space to jump. Press F to open photo mode. Fire, animals and audio freeze…

Action Keyboard / Mouse Gamepad Toggle photo mode F View / Share Move / fly WASD, Space up, Left Ctrl down Left stick, triggers up/down Look Hold right mouse button Right stick Sprint Left Shift Left stick click Switch UI category Q / E LB / RB Roll Z / C Roll slider (Camera category) Zoom (FOV) Mouse wheel FOV slider (Camera category) Navigate UI Mouse / arrow keys D-pad, A to press Hide UI H Y…
Core Concepts : the architecture in five minutes. Events & Scripting : drive photo mode from code. Theming the UI : make it look like your game.
Photo Mode Pro is built around one component, one settings asset and a handful of small, replaceable pieces. This page explains how they fit together so the how-to guides make sense at a glance.

PhotoModeController (menu: Photo Mode Pro/Photo Mode Controller ) is the single entry point. Add it to any GameObject (or drop in the generated prefab), assign a PhotoModeSettings asset, and you are done. The component is marked [DisallowMultipleComponent] , and if a second controller appears in the scene it disables itself with a warning: there is always at most one active controller. Scripts…

EnterPhotoMode() (or the configured toggle binding, F / View by default) does, in order: Records the current Time.timeScale , AudioListener.pause , cursor visibility, cursor lock state and the main camera's enabled flag. Pauses the game per PhotoModeSettings.pauseMode ( TimeScaleZero by default, or Unscaled to keep the game running) and audio per audioPauseMode . Clones the camera tagged…

PhotoModeController.Adjustments is a plain PhotoModeAdjustments object holding every image tweak in natural units . No pipeline types anywhere: Field Unit / range Neutral exposureEv EV, −3 .. +3 0 contrast , saturation , temperature −100 .. +100 0 vignette , filmGrain 0 .. 1 0 depthOfFieldEnabled bool off focusDistance meters 10 aperture f-number (applied clamped to f/0.7–f/16) f/5.6 Values are…

Adjustments are pushed through IPhotoModeEffectsBackend (namespace PhotoModePro.Effects ), a small render-pipeline abstraction. Four implementations ship in the box: URP volume, HDRP volume (physical-camera depth of field), Post Processing Stack v2, and a minimal Built-in fallback shader. Selection is automatic from the active render pipeline; force one via PhotoModeSettings.backendOverride if…

PhotoModeSettings ( Assets > Create > Photo Mode Pro > Settings ) holds every tunable: key/gamepad bindings, pause and audio behavior, free-camera limits (speed, sphere clamp, collision, FOV range, roll speed), the effects backend override, capture defaults and the save path, and visibility options. Duplicate it per configuration and assign it on the controller, or swap it from script via the…

A PhotoModePreset asset is just a named bundle of adjustment values. Six ship built in (Clean, Noir, Vivid, Warm, Cool, Vintage); the controller's Presets list decides what the Presets tab offers. ApplyPreset(preset) copies the values in; ApplyPreset(null) resets to the neutral "Clean" state. See Presets .

The photo mode UI is an ordinary UGUI prefab ( PhotoModeUIController ) that the controller instantiates on enter and destroys on exit. It has four categories (Camera, Effects, Presets, Capture) cycled with Q/E or LB/RB , full keyboard/gamepad navigation, and a single row of hotkey hint chips along the bottom edge of the screen. Every color, sprite, font, motion parameter and UI sound comes from…

CapturePhoto() (or P / the SNAP button) renders the photo camera into a RenderTexture at 1×, 2× or 4× the screen resolution ( Supersample ), with temporal anti-aliasing and dynamic resolution disabled for the shot so the output is artifact-free and exactly the requested size. The result is read back with AsyncGPUReadback where the platform supports it, encoded to PNG with a timestamped…

When photo mode opens, the main camera is cloned into a free photo camera driven by a FreeCameraController . It runs on unscaled time (so it works while the game is frozen) and allocates nothing per frame.

Mode Behavior Fly (default) Free flight: WASD / left stick moves, Space / Left Ctrl (triggers) elevate, mouse / right stick looks. Orbit The camera circles the activation point (where photo mode was opened) at an adjustable distance. Look input orbits; forward/back input changes the orbit distance. Switch in the UI's Camera tab (Fly / Orbit buttons) or from script. Switching preserves the current…

Action Keyboard / Mouse Gamepad Move / fly WASD, Space up, Left Ctrl down Left stick, triggers up/down Look Hold right mouse button Right stick Sprint Left Shift Left stick click Roll Z / C Roll slider (Camera tab) Zoom (FOV) Mouse wheel FOV slider (Camera tab) Notes: Sprint multiplies both move speed and roll speed by PhotoModeSettings.sprintMultiplier . Wheel zoom only acts when the pointer is…

Both are configured on the settings asset : Setting Meaning maxDistance Max distance from the activation point, in meters (sphere clamp). Default 15; 0 = unlimited. enableCollision Sphere-cast the camera against geometry so it cannot clip through walls. Off by default. collisionRadius Radius of that sphere cast (default 0.2 m). collisionMask Layers the camera collides with. Note The sphere clamp…

PhotoModeController.PhotoCamera returns the FreeCameraController while photo mode is active, and null otherwise: using PhotoModePro ; using UnityEngine ; FreeCameraController cam = PhotoModeController . Instance . PhotoCamera ; if ( cam != null ) { cam . Mode = PhotoModeCameraMode . Orbit ; // or call cam.SetMode(...) cam . Fov = 35f ; // clamped to settings.minFov..maxFov cam . Roll = 10f ; //…

The Effects tab exposes the full adjustment set. Every value lives in PhotoModeController.Adjustments (a PhotoModeAdjustments object) in natural, pipeline-agnostic units, and is sticky for the play session.

Control Field Unit / range Notes Exposure exposureEv EV, −3 .. +3 Relative exposure compensation; identical semantics on every pipeline (on HDRP it maps to post-exposure, so it never fights auto-exposure). Contrast contrast −100 .. +100 0 is neutral. Saturation saturation −100 .. +100 −100 is grayscale. Temperature temperature −100 (cool) .. +100 (warm) 0 is neutral. Vignette vignette 0 .. 1 Edge…

The aperture slider spans f/0.7 – f/16 with a perceptual log-stop mapping: each step of slider travel multiplies the f-number by a constant ratio, so a nudge near f/1 changes the bokeh about as much as a nudge near f/11, the way photographers think in stops. Lower f-numbers mean a shallower focus plane and bigger bokeh; f/0.7 is the widest aperture HDRP's physical camera accepts, and beyond ~f/16…

Not every backend supports every effect. The Built-in fallback (no Post Processing Stack v2) lacks temperature, grain and depth of field, and HDRP cannot do transparent captures. Unsupported controls hide automatically , and scripts can check the same flags: using PhotoModePro ; using PhotoModePro.Effects ; bool hasDof = ( PhotoModeController . Instance . ActiveCapabilities & EffectCapabilities …

Write the fields directly, then tell the controller you did. That marks the effects dirty (re-applied next frame) and flips the preset label to "Custom": using PhotoModePro ; var controller = PhotoModeController . Instance ; controller . Adjustments . exposureEv = 0.5f ; controller . Adjustments . saturation = - 100f ; // grayscale controller . Adjustments . vignette = 0.35f ; controller …

A preset is a PhotoModePreset asset: a display name plus one full set of adjustment values . There are no LUT textures or shaders involved, just plain data, so presets stay tiny and diff cleanly in version control.

Preset Look Clean Neutral: every value at its default. First in the list and the active default. Noir High-contrast desaturated black & white. Vivid Punchy contrast and saturation. Warm Golden-hour temperature shift. Cool Blue-shifted, moody. Vintage Faded grain-and-vignette film look. Clean is the default state : PhotoModeController.CurrentPresetLabel reads "Clean" when photo mode opens and…

Assets > Create > Photo Mode Pro > Preset . Set displayName , the label shown on the Presets tab button. Fill in the adjustments block (same fields and units as the Effects tab : EV, −100..+100, 0..1, meters, f-stops). Author presets live Enter Play mode, dial in a look with the sliders, then copy the values into your preset asset. The component Inspector and the asset use identical fields, so it…

The controller's Presets list (Inspector field, or the Presets property from script) decides which presets the Presets tab shows, in order: using PhotoModePro ; var controller = PhotoModeController . Instance ; controller . Presets . Add ( myPreset ); // List<PhotoModePreset> The generated prefab ships with the built-in six already assigned; change the list however you like.

using PhotoModePro ; var controller = PhotoModeController . Instance ; controller . ApplyPreset ( myPreset ); // copies values in, re-applies effects controller . ApplyPreset ( null ); // programmatic reset -> neutral "Clean" state ApplyPreset copies the preset's values into controller.Adjustments , pushes them to the effects backend and raises OnAdjustmentsReplaced (so the UI sliders re-seed)…

t: The label: Clean, a preset name, or "Custom"
b: CurrentPresetLabel tracks where the current look came from: State Label Neutral (open, or after ApplyPreset(null) ) "Clean" A preset was applied the preset's displayName Any slider moved (or NotifyAdjustmentsEdited() called) after that "Custom" The label is sticky for the play session, like the adjustment values themselves. The UI shows it so players always know whether they are on a stock look…

Press P (keyboard), X / Square (gamepad) or the SNAP button, or call PhotoModeController.CapturePhoto() from script. The UI hides itself for the shot, the frame is rendered offscreen, encoded to PNG and saved to disk, and a thumbnail toast confirms it.

The Capture tab offers 1× / 2× / 4× output resolution ( PhotoModeController.Supersample , default from PhotoModeSettings.defaultSupersample ). A 4× capture of a 1080p view renders at 7680×4320. Temporal anti-aliasing and dynamic resolution are disabled for the duration of every capture, so the output is artifact-free and exactly the requested size. Heavy captures show a badge Supersampled…

Setting Result PhotoModeSettings.customSavePath empty (default) {Application.persistentDataPath}/Photos customSavePath set that directory, used verbatim PhotoModeController.SaveDirectory returns the resolved directory at runtime. The save-path row on the Capture tab is clickable and opens the folder; clicking the capture toast reveals the new file (Windows Explorer reveal; folder open on other…

Toggle Transparent BG on the Capture tab (or set PhotoModeController.TransparentCapture ) to capture with a clean alpha channel, useful for compositing screenshots over other art. Warning Transparent frames render without post-processing (post effects destroy alpha), so your image adjustments do not appear in transparent shots. Not available on HDRP : the toggle is hidden there and a transparent…

Toggle Hotkey What it does Hide UI H / Y (Triangle) Hides the photo mode chrome for a clean preview. Capture feedback (flash, toast) stays visible. Hide Game UI Capture tab Hides the canvases registered on the controller ( GameUiCanvases list + gameUiTag ). On by default when autoHideGameUi is set. Hide Player Capture tab Disables the renderers of the registered player objects ( PlayerObjects…

using PhotoModePro ; using UnityEngine ; void OnEnable () { PhotoModeController . Instance . OnPhotoCaptured += HandlePhoto ; } void HandlePhoto ( string path , Texture2D thumbnail ) { Debug . Log ( $"Photo saved to {path}" ); // e.g. push the thumbnail into your gallery UI, or upload the file. } The same event exists as a UnityEvent ( On Photo Captured ) on the controller for Inspector wiring…
Everything in the UI is also available from script. The whole public surface lives on PhotoModeController (namespace PhotoModePro ), reached via the null-safe static PhotoModeController.Instance .

C# event Signature Fires OnEntered Action Photo mode entered. OnExited Action Photo mode exited (state already restored). OnPhotoCaptured Action<string, Texture2D> A photo was saved (path and thumbnail). OnAdjustmentsReplaced Action The adjustment values were replaced wholesale (preset applied or reset). Three of these also exist as serialized UnityEvents on the component for Inspector-only…

t: Enter / exit from a pause menu
b: EnterPhotoMode() , ExitPhotoMode() and TogglePhotoMode() are plain public methods. Wire them to a button or call them from your game-state machine: using PhotoModePro ; public void OnPhotoModeButtonClicked () { pauseMenu . Hide (); PhotoModeController . Instance . EnterPhotoMode (); } void Awake () { // Reopen the pause menu when the player leaves photo mode. PhotoModeController . Instance …

If a photo mode binding doubles as a gameplay action (the demo's Space is both fly-up and jump), the closing key press would otherwise bleed into gameplay on the exit frame. ExitedThisFrame is true only during that single frame: void Update () { var photoMode = PhotoModeController . Instance ; if ( photoMode != null && ( photoMode . IsActive || photoMode . ExitedThisFrame )) return ; // photo…

t: Audio: mixer snapshots instead of AudioListener.pause
b: By default photo mode sets AudioListener.pause on enter and restores the prior value exactly on exit. If your game drives audio through mixer snapshots, set PhotoModeSettings.audioPauseMode to AudioPauseMode.None (the package then never touches audio) and transition in your own handlers: using PhotoModePro ; using UnityEngine.Audio ; [SerializeField] AudioMixerSnapshot pausedSnapshot …

ActiveCapabilities returns the EffectCapabilities flags of the active effects backend. While photo mode is closed, it returns a cheap, side-effect-free prediction of the backend that would be selected right now: using PhotoModePro ; using PhotoModePro.Effects ; EffectCapabilities caps = PhotoModeController . Instance . ActiveCapabilities ; if (( caps & EffectCapabilities . DepthOfField ) != 0 ) {…

Member Purpose Instance Null-safe static accessor to the active controller. IsActive / IsCapturing / ExitedThisFrame State queries. EnterPhotoMode() / ExitPhotoMode() / TogglePhotoMode() Lifecycle. Adjustments + NotifyAdjustmentsEdited() Image adjustments . ApplyPreset(preset) / Presets / CurrentPresetLabel Presets . CapturePhoto() / Supersample / TransparentCapture / SaveDirectory Capture …
The entire photo mode UI (every panel, control, label, animation and sound) restyles from a single PhotoModeUITheme asset. Swap the asset to ship a new look.

Duplicate the shipped PhotoModeUITheme.asset (or create a fresh one via Assets > Create > Photo Mode Pro > UI Theme ). Change colors, sprites, fonts, motion and audio on the duplicate. Assign it to the Theme field on the PhotoModeUIController component of your photo mode UI prefab. The theme is applied at bind time (every time the UI spawns), so the next time photo mode opens, the whole panel…

Colors ¶ Field Used for panelColor Main panel background. controlColor Slider/button/toggle backgrounds. accentColor Fills, highlights, the selected tab. textColor Primary labels. dimTextColor Hints and value readouts. Sprites and icons ¶ panelSprite , controlSprite and handleSprite override the builder defaults (null keeps them). The generated 9-sliced sprites ( roundedPanelSprite …

The theme restyles the photo mode panel and its floating chrome (toolbar, hint chips, toast). It does not touch your game's own UI, and gameplay HUD canvases are only ever hidden/shown by photo mode, never restyled (see Capturing photos for the visibility toggles). Related: Core Concepts · FAQ

Photo Mode Pro is built to drop into an existing game without taking it over. It requires no packages beyond Unity itself and does not edit your ProjectSettings; state is restored exactly on exit. This page covers the common integration points.

Photo Mode Pro has no hard dependency on Cinemachine (the package compiles and runs without it) but plays cleanly with it when present (the Setup Wizard detects and reports Cinemachine in its checks). What happens on enter when a CinemachineBrain drives your main camera: The main camera (carrying the brain) is disabled , which suspends Cinemachine's hold on the rendered view. The brain component…

The input layer is abstracted behind version defines and works with all three Active Input Handling settings (the Setup Wizard verifies your configuration): Project setting Status Input System Package (new) Recommended. Full keyboard, mouse and gamepad support. Input Manager (legacy) Supported, with the gamepad limitation below. Both Supported; the new Input System backend is used. Legacy Input…

PhotoModeSettings.audioPauseMode picks the strategy: Mode Behavior AudioListenerPause (default) AudioListener.pause is set on enter; the prior value is restored exactly on exit, so a game that was already paused stays paused. None Photo mode never touches audio. Drive your own transitions from the enter/exit events. The mixer-snapshot pattern with None : using PhotoModePro ; using…

Treat photo mode as one more state your game can be in, owned by your state machine: using PhotoModePro ; public class GameStateMachine : MonoBehaviour { void Start () { var photoMode = PhotoModeController . Instance ; photoMode . OnEntered += () => SetState ( GameState . PhotoMode ); photoMode . OnExited += () => SetState ( GameState . Paused ); // back to the menu } public void…

Photo Mode Pro detects the active render pipeline at runtime and picks the right effects backend automatically. Controls for effects the active backend cannot apply hide themselves ( PhotoModeController.ActiveCapabilities exposes the same flags to scripts).

Effect URP HDRP Built-in (PPv2) Built-in (fallback) Exposure ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ Contrast ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ Saturation ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ Temperature ✓ ✓ ✓ — Vignette ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ Film grain ✓ ✓ ✓ — Depth of field ✓ ✓ ✓ — Transparent capture ✓ — ✓ ✓

Effects run through a code-built global Volume at very high priority. No scene assets are touched, and everything is removed on exit. URP's film grain reads weaker than PPv2's at the same intensity, so the URP backend uses a coarse grain lookup ( Large01 ) with a low luminance response to match the PPv2 look. URP's own depth-of-field aperture parameter floors at f/1.0 , so the bottom of the…

Depth of field drives the physical camera ( Camera.aperture , focalLength ); the package keeps physical properties enabled while applying adjustments so FOV smoothing can't silently kill DoF. Exposure maps to ColorAdjustments.postExposure (relative EV, same semantics as URP) rather than HDRP's absolute scene Exposure override, so the slider behaves identically across pipelines and never fights…

t: Built-in with Post Processing Stack v2
b: The backend creates its own PostProcessVolume , profile and PostProcessLayer on the photo camera, giving the full adjustment set. PPv2's vignette reads weaker than URP's at the same intensity, so the slider value is boosted internally toward the URP look; expect subtle character differences between pipelines at identical slider values.

A single internal full-screen shader provides exposure, contrast, saturation and vignette. Temperature, film grain and depth of field are unavailable and their controls hide automatically. Install com.unity.postprocessing for the full set; the Setup Wizard's effects-backend check links you straight to the Package Manager.

The aperture property accepts any positive value, but backends clamp it into f/0.7–f/16 when applying ( the effects how-to explains why ). Out-of-range values from old presets or scripts are clamped and never raise an error.

Demo materials are generated for the active pipeline (Standard, URP Lit or HDRP Lit). Opening the demo scene under a mismatched pipeline offers Rebuild demo for current pipeline ; the Setup Wizard's demo check offers the same rebuild. See Troubleshooting if the demo ever renders pink.
Unity Status 2021.3 LTS Supported (verified, URP 12) 2022.3 LTS Supported (verified, URP 14) 6000.x Supported (verified, URP 17 / HDRP 17 / Built-in)

Pipeline Status URP 12 / 14 / 17 Fully supported HDRP 12+ Fully supported (no transparent capture; see Per-Pipeline Notes ) Built-in + Post Processing Stack v2 Fully supported Built-in without PPv2 Supported with the minimal fallback backend (exposure, contrast, saturation, vignette) Custom SRPs Not supported (the fallback backend and Standard-shader demo materials are used; no claims made)

Input handling Status Input System (new) Fully supported: keyboard, mouse, gamepad incl. UI navigation Legacy Input Manager Supported: keyboard + mouse fully; gamepad right-stick look and triggers unavailable (the default InputManager.asset defines no axes for them and the package does not edit ProjectSettings) Both Fully supported (verified)

Platform Status Windows / macOS / Linux standalone Fully supported (Mono and IL2CPP; IL2CPP verified with Managed Stripping Level = High) WebGL Not supported in this version (capture-to-disk); known limitation, roadmap item Mobile (touch UI) Untested / roadmap (no touch UI yet) Consoles Untested / roadmap: the gamepad input layer is console-style, but no platform claims are made

Zero per-frame managed (GC) allocations while photo mode is open and idle and while adjusting sliders (after the first-open warmup frame, which builds caches). The idle paths are enforced by allocation gates in the automated test suite on every release run rather than measured once. While closed , the controller only polls the toggle binding. There is no other per-frame work and nothing…

Measured in the demo scene (Unity 6000.3, mid-range desktop, 640×480 base render in the automated batch run). Informational, not a contract: Supersample Wall time 1× ≈ 60–250 ms 2× ≈ 180–270 ms 4× ≈ 700–880 ms Times scale roughly with output pixel count (a 4× capture of a 1080p view renders at 7680×4320) and are dominated by the GPU readback and PNG encode; expect multiple seconds at 4× from…

Repeated open/close cycles are leak-gated in the test suite: zero net scene-object growth and zero Unity-object growth over 100 cycles. The UI prefab is instantiated on first open and reused afterwards.

Prefer 2× over 4× for social-media-sized output; 4× exists for print-quality shots. Transparent captures render without post-processing (clean alpha), which also makes them slightly cheaper. See Capture for the full capture pipeline.
Quick answers to the questions buyers ask most. For symptom-driven help, see Troubleshooting ; for version migrations, see the Upgrade Guide .

How long does it take to add photo mode to my game? ¶ About 15 minutes. Drag the generated PhotoModePro.prefab into your scene, add your HUD canvases and player root(s) on the PhotoModeController component, and press your toggle key in Play mode. The Quick Start walks through every step. What does the Setup Wizard do? ¶ Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Setup Wizard runs green/yellow/red checks on your…

Which Unity versions and render pipelines are supported? ¶ Unity 2021.3 LTS or newer , verified on 2021.3, 2022.3 and 6000.3. All three render pipelines: URP (12+) , HDRP (12+) and the Built-in Render Pipeline (full set with Post Processing Stack v2, minimal fallback without it). The right effects backend is detected automatically at runtime. See the Support Matrix and Pipelines . Does it work…

Where are photos saved? ¶ {Application.persistentDataPath}/Photos by default, as timestamped, collision-safe PNGs. Override the folder via PhotoModeSettings.customSavePath . In-game, clicking the capture toast (or the save-path row on the CAPTURE tab) opens the folder (on Windows with the new photo selected). See Capture . Why is the Transparent BG toggle missing on HDRP? ¶ HDRP cannot produce…

Can I rebind the keys? ¶ Yes. The toggle, hide-UI and capture hotkeys (and pause behavior, camera limits, save folder and more) live on the PhotoModeSettings asset. Duplicate it or create one via Assets > Create > Photo Mode Pro > Settings . When you change a hotkey in the Inspector, an editor dialog offers to update the matching on-screen hint chips for you. Can I restyle the UI to match my…

Why does the demo render pink/magenta? ¶ The demo materials were generated for a different render pipeline than the one your project is using. Since 1.4.0 the demo is pipeline-aware: opening the demo scene under a mismatched pipeline offers "Rebuild demo for current pipeline" . Accept it, or run Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Build Demo Assets manually. See Troubleshooting . Can I delete the Demo…

Symptom-driven fixes, including every historical bug fixed in 1.2.x–1.4.0. First steps for almost anything: update to the latest version, run Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Setup Wizard (one-click fixes for pipeline, input, TMP and demo-material problems), and run Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Run Self-Test . Version check Many entries below were bugs fixed in a specific release. The current version is…

Symptom: The demo scene (or its props) shows pink/magenta materials. Cause: The demo materials were generated for a different render pipeline than the one currently active — pink is Unity's "shader not supported" color. Fix: Since 1.4.0 the demo is pipeline-aware: materials are generated for the active pipeline, and opening the demo scene under a mismatched pipeline offers "Rebuild demo for…

t: TextMeshPro text is missing or shows boxes
b: Symptom: UI labels are invisible or render as empty squares. Cause: The TMP Essential Resources are not imported in your project. The photo mode UI uses TextMeshPro. Fix: Import them once via Window > TextMeshPro > Import TMP Essential Resources , or use the Setup Wizard's one-click fix (it checks TMP essentials). The demo builder imports them automatically.

Symptom: You worry the UI needs an EventSystem your scene doesn't have (or, on old versions, the panel didn't respond to input without one). Cause: UGUI input requires an EventSystem; not every game scene has one. Fix: Fixed in 1.3.0; update the package. Photo mode now creates an EventSystem on open if the scene has none (matching your active input handling), logs a single info message, and…

t: Photo mode opens but the game keeps running
b: Symptom: Entering photo mode doesn't freeze gameplay. Cause: The pause behavior on your PhotoModeSettings asset is set to the non-pausing "unscaled" mode. Or your gameplay code runs on unscaled time, so timeScale = 0 doesn't affect it. Fix: Check the pause behavior on the settings asset assigned to your PhotoModeController (the default pauses via timeScale = 0 ). If your game loop uses unscaled…

t: Audio doesn't resume (or doesn't pause) after photo mode
b: Symptom: Game audio stays paused after exiting photo mode, or never pauses on enter, typically in games that drive audio through mixer snapshots. Cause: PhotoModeSettings.audioPauseMode controls this. The default, AudioListenerPause , sets AudioListener.pause on enter and restores the prior value exactly on exit; if your own code also writes AudioListener.pause , or your audio runs through…

t: Gamepad navigation goes dead after a mouse click
b: Symptom: Arrow keys / D-pad do nothing when photo mode opens, or stop working after clicking a control with the mouse, until you switch tabs with Q/E. (NB-01) Cause: Mouse clicks clear the EventSystem selection by design (so the focus ring never lingers for mouse users); older versions had no recovery path, so navigation lost its anchor. Fix: Fixed in 1.3.0. Update the package. The first control…

t: Gamepad right stick or triggers don't work
b: Symptom: Right-stick look and trigger fly up/down do nothing on gamepad. Cause: You are on the legacy Input Manager. The default InputManager.asset defines no axes for the right stick or triggers, and the package never edits ProjectSettings. This is a documented limitation rather than a bug. Fix: Install and enable the Input System package (recommended), or add the missing axes to your own…

t: Focus rings are cut off at panel edges
b: Symptom: The thin focus ring around toggles, preset buttons, Fly/Orbit, 1x/2x/4x or the capture-folder row is clipped by the side panel's scroll mask. (NB-03–NB-07, NB-09, NB-10; regressed and re-fixed as NB-16, NB-17) Cause: Focus rings extend a few pixels beyond each control; without enough inner content padding the scroll viewport's mask clips them. The 1.4.0 regression had a subtler cause…

t: Keyboard/gamepad selection scrolls out of view
b: Symptom: Arrowing down a long tab (e.g. Effects) moves the selection below the visible scroll area and the panel doesn't follow. (NB-02) Cause: Older versions had no scroll-follow logic for EventSystem selection changes. Fix: Fixed in 1.3.0; update the package. The panel now auto-scrolls minimally, just enough to reveal the selected control plus its focus ring. It never moves when the selection…

t: The highlight overlaps neighboring controls or hides toggle checkmarks
b: Symptom: A soft blue glow bleeds past controls, overlaps the bottom menu / presets / 1x-2x-4x row, lingers after mouse clicks, hides toggle checkmarks, or sits on the wrong supersample button. (Phase 2 items 2–5, 13, 27) Cause: The original expanding soft-glow highlight extended 8–12 px past controls and persisted after clicks. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. The glow was replaced by a…

t: The Effects panel overflows / mouse wheel zooms instead of scrolling
b: Symptom: Long tabs run off the panel with no way to scroll, and the mouse wheel zooms the camera even when the pointer is over the UI. (Phase 2 items 1, 9) Cause: Pre-1.2.1 the side-panel pages had no scroll view and wheel input went to camera zoom unconditionally. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. All pages live in a shared ScrollRect with a slim scrollbar; the wheel scrolls the panel…

t: The aperture slider seems to do nothing above f/3
b: Symptom: Dragging the aperture slider up changes bokeh only at the low end; most of the travel produces no visible difference. (NB-15) Cause: The old slider range extended far past visually useful f-stops, mapped linearly, so almost all the travel sat in a perceptual dead zone. Fix: Fixed in 1.4.0. Update the package. The slider is re-ranged to f/0.7–f/16 with a perceptual log-stop mapping, so…

t: The Focus Dist slider seems to do nothing above ~3 m
b: Symptom: Focus distance visibly pulls only at the low end of the slider. (Phase 2 item 15) Cause: The slider mapped 0.1–100 m linearly; the visibly focus-pulling 0.1–3 m range occupied a sliver of the travel. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1; update the package. The slider now uses a logarithmic mapping (half the travel covers 0.1–3 m); the readout stays in real meters.

t: Focus Dist and Aperture rows show while Depth of Field is off
b: Symptom: The DoF sub-controls remain visible with the DoF toggle off. (Phase 2 item 16) Cause: A UI bug. The rendered blur deactivated correctly, but the rows did not hide. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package.

t: Slider changes don't show in HDRP
b: Symptom: On HDRP, the Focus Dist and Aperture sliders have no effect. (Phase 2 items 37, 38) Cause: FOV smoothing wrote Camera.fieldOfView every frame, which silently resets usePhysicalProperties , so HDRP's physical-camera depth of field never saw the values. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. The camera is now driven via focalLength while physical properties are in use. If a custom camera…

t: Film grain or vignette looks different across pipelines
b: Symptom: The same slider value reads much stronger or weaker on one pipeline (originally: grain far stronger on PPv2 than URP, vignette far weaker on PPv2 than URP). (Phase 2 items 30, 31) Cause: The underlying post stacks respond differently to identical parameter values. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. URP grain uses a coarse lookup with low luminance response to match the PPv2 look…

t: Effect controls are missing on the Built-in pipeline
b: Symptom: Temperature, film grain and depth of field don't appear in the UI on Built-in. Cause: com.unity.postprocessing (Post Processing Stack v2) is not installed, so the minimal fallback backend is active. It supports exposure, contrast, saturation and vignette only, and unsupported controls hide automatically. Fix: Install Post Processing Stack v2 for the full adjustment set. The Setup…

t: The Transparent BG toggle is missing on HDRP
b: Symptom: No transparent-capture option appears on HDRP. Cause: By design. Clean alpha would require an alpha-capable color buffer format on the HDRP asset, which the package does not edit; the capability is reported as unsupported and the toggle hides. Fix: None on HDRP. URP and both Built-in backends support transparent capture. See the FAQ and Pipelines .

t: Captured PNG has UI in it
b: Symptom: Your game's HUD appears in captured photos. Cause: Photo mode hides only the canvases listed in the controller's Game Ui Canvases field; a HUD canvas not listed there (especially world-space or camera-space canvases the photo camera can see) renders into the shot. Fix: Add your HUD canvases to Game Ui Canvases on the PhotoModeController ; they are hidden on enter and restored exactly on…

t: Capture flash and thumbnail don't appear with the UI hidden
b: Symptom: With Hide UI active, capturing works but you get no white flash, shutter feedback or thumbnail toast. (Phase 2 item 39) Cause: Pre-1.2.1, Hide UI disabled the whole root canvas and took the capture feedback down with it. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. Capture feedback (flash, shutter sound, toast) is now exempt from Hide UI on every pipeline; only the chrome (toolbar, panel…

t: Capturing at 4x freezes the game with no feedback
b: Symptom: A 4x capture blocks for seconds and nothing indicates progress. (Phase 2 item 10) Cause: Supersampled captures render synchronously on the main thread; older versions showed nothing during the block. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. 2x/4x captures now show a static "Processing…" badge for the duration (it never appears in the photo). The wall-time itself is expected — a 4x…

t: Photo mode won't exit right after pressing capture
b: Symptom: Pressing the toggle immediately after capturing does nothing for a moment. Cause: By design: ExitPhotoMode() no-ops while a capture's async GPU readback is in flight (a few frames), to avoid tearing down the photo camera mid-readback. Fix: Wait the fraction of a second; the exit then proceeds normally.

t: Captures look slightly different from the on-screen image
b: Symptom: A capture is crisper than the live view, or you expected TAA in the shot. Cause: By design. Temporal anti-aliasing (URP and HDRP) is disabled for the duration of every capture and dynamic resolution is forced off, because TAA history is invalid for an offscreen render and produces ghosting/jitter. MSAA/SMAA/FXAA are unaffected. Fix: Nothing to fix. This guarantees artifact-free output at…

t: Player pops or jumps after Hide Player
b: Symptom: Toggle Hide Player on and off, exit photo mode, and the character jumps or pops on return to gameplay. (NB-13) Cause: Older versions hid the player with SetActive(false/true) ; re-activation reset CharacterController grounding and rebound the Animator. Additionally, a shared binding (e.g. Space as both fly-up and jump) could bleed into gameplay on the closing frame. Fix: Fixed in 1.3.0…

t: The demo character shrinks or scales up during jumps
b: Symptom: The demo adventurer briefly becomes tiny then scales back up while jumping, or after Hide Player. (Phase 2 items 20, 22) Cause: Imported animation clips keyed m_LocalScale , which snapped the model back to FBX units during the airborne state and on Animator rebind. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1; update the package. The animator now uses de-scaled copies of the clips. If it persists after updating…

t: Hint chips overlap my game's UI (or look stacked/cramped)
b: Symptom: The hotkey hint chips collide with other UI, or pile up as a tall stack in the bottom-left corner. Earlier: the chips were too small to read. (NB-11, NB-12, NB-14) Cause: The chips were originally undersized (fixed in 1.3.0 with a ×1.5 scale-up into four short rows), and the 1.3.0 four-row block read as a cramped stack at some window sizes. Fix: Fixed in 1.4.0; update the package. The…

t: Hint chips show the wrong keys after rebinding
b: Symptom: You changed hotkeys on the settings asset but the on-screen hints still show the old keys. (Phase 2 item 12) Cause: Pre-1.2.1, hint text was not updated on hotkey changes. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. Changing a hotkey in the Inspector now offers (via an editor dialog) to update the matching hint chips in the UI prefab. If you declined the dialog or use a moved/duplicated…

t: The NONE preset button is missing
b: Symptom: The Presets tab no longer has a NONE entry. Cause: By design since 1.3.0: NONE was visually indistinguishable from Clean , so it was removed. Clean is now the first preset and the active default. Fix: Nothing to fix. ApplyPreset(null) remains the programmatic reset and maps to the same state, with no API break. See Presets .

t: Clicking the capture toast does nothing
b: Symptom: The thumbnail toast after a capture isn't clickable. (Phase 2 item 6) Cause: Pre-1.2.1, the toast blocked no raycasts. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. Clicking the toast opens the file browser with the new photo selected (Explorer reveal on Windows; folder open elsewhere). The save-path row on the CAPTURE tab is clickable too.

t: The FOV slider ignores my Min/Max FOV settings
b: Symptom: PhotoModeSettings.minFov/maxFov limit wheel zoom but the FOV slider still runs 10–120. (Phase 2 item 14) Cause: Pre-1.2.1, the slider range was hardcoded. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. The slider range is read from the settings asset at UI bind time.

t: Sprint doesn't speed up camera roll
b: Symptom: Holding Shift speeds up movement but Z/C roll stays slow. (Phase 2 item 8) Cause: Pre-1.2.1, the sprint multiplier applied to movement only. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1; update the package. Roll now uses the same sprintMultiplier . (1.2.1 also added the missing "Space/Ctrl · Up/Down" and "Z/C · Roll" hint chips, Phase 2 item 7.)

t: Hint text is hard to read over bright surfaces
b: Symptom: Floating hint labels, toolbar Q/E hints or the toast filename wash out over white/bright backgrounds; the demo HUD looked like plain text. (Phase 2 items 21, 25) Cause: Scene-floating text had no outline, and the old demo HUD was a single legacy Text element. Fix: Fixed in 1.2.1. Update the package. Scene-floating text now gets a thin black TMP outline + underlay, and the demo HUD is…

t: I can't find a camera collision toggle in the UI
b: Symptom: Camera collision can't be switched in-game. Cause: By design. Collision is a settings-asset-only option ( Enable Collision on PhotoModeSettings ) with no in-game toggle. Fix: Set it on the settings asset. See Camera .

t: My forced backend override is ignored
b: Symptom: You set PhotoModeSettings.backendOverride but a different backend runs, with a warning in the console. Cause: The forced backend is unavailable in the project (e.g. forcing Hdrp in a URP project, or PostProcessingV2 without the package installed). An unavailable forced backend logs one warning and falls back to Auto. Fix: Install/activate the matching pipeline or package, or leave the…

t: The Profiler shows GC allocations while photo mode is open
b: Symptom: With the Profiler attached in the editor, sliders or idle photo mode appear to allocate a little every frame, despite the zero-GC claim. Cause: Editor-only instrumentation. Probes show the bytes come from uGUI/TMP editor profiler markers and the measuring recorder itself: uGUI's Slider.Set alone allocates ~123 B/frame in editor play mode with no package code involved. The package's own…

Run Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Run Self-Test . It writes PhotoModeSelfTest.json with your environment and check results. Use Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Report a Bug (or email support.dan.max@gmail.com ) and attach PhotoModeSelfTest.json , plus your Unity version, render pipeline and reproduction steps. The FAQ covers intentional behaviors that are sometimes mistaken for bugs, and the Support Matrix…
All notable changes to Photo Mode Pro are documented here. The format follows Keep a Changelog .

Added ¶ Setup Wizard (Tools → Photo Mode Pro → Setup Wizard): green/yellow/red project checks (render pipeline, effects backend, input handling, Cinemachine, TextMesh Pro essentials, demo materials) with one-click fixes where automatable, and an Add Photo Mode To Scene button that drops the configured prefab into the open scene and selects it. Editor menu root Tools → Photo Mode Pro: Setup…

Added ¶ Unity 2021.3+ support. The minimum Unity version drops from 6000.0 to 2021.3 LTS; the package is verified on 2021.3, 2022.3 and 6000.3, on URP 12/14/17, Built-in (PPv2 and fallback) and HDRP, with the new Input System, the legacy Input Manager and Active Input Handling = Both. PhotoModeSettings.audioPauseMode ( AudioPauseMode.AudioListenerPause / AudioPauseMode.None ): with None the…

Fixed ¶ All side-panel pages now live in a shared ScrollRect with a slim scrollbar; the mouse wheel scrolls the panel when the pointer is over UI and zooms the camera otherwise. The expanding soft-glow control highlight is replaced by a thin (~2 px) snug outline ring (circular on slider knobs and toggles, rounded-rect on buttons and tabs) that never overlaps neighboring controls or hides the…

Added ¶ Built-in Render Pipeline support : a Post Processing Stack v2 backend (own PostProcessVolume + profile + layer on the photo camera, full adjustment set) when com.unity.postprocessing is installed, and a minimal fallback backend (exposure / contrast / saturation / vignette via one internal full-screen shader) when it is not. HDRP support : an HDRP volume backend (ColorAdjustments…

Added ¶ Demo upgrade: the primitive corridor is replaced by a stylized forest-clearing showcase scene (CC0 third-party assets, provenance in Demo/ThirdParty/ATTRIBUTIONS.md ), still built 100% programmatically by DemoSceneBuilder . Rigged, animated low-poly adventurer with a from-scratch third-person controller ( ThirdPersonCharacter ): camera-relative WASD / left-stick movement, walk/run sprint…

Added ¶ Photo mode controller with EnterPhotoMode() / ExitPhotoMode() public API, static PhotoModeController.Instance accessor, C# events and UnityEvent mirrors ( OnEntered , OnExited , OnPhotoCaptured(path, thumbnail) ). Configurable pause behavior: timeScale = 0 with audio pause (default) or non-pausing "unscaled" mode. Free photo camera with fly and orbit modes, sphere clamping around the…

How to move an existing project to a newer Photo Mode Pro version, newest first. Each step lists what changed, who is affected, and what (if anything) you must do. The full change list lives in the Changelog . After any upgrade Let Unity reimport the package fully. Run Tools > Photo Mode Pro > Run Self-Test . It writes PhotoModeSelfTest.json and verifies your setup end to end. If you ship a…

No breaking API changes. Two behavioral changes and new editor tooling: Aperture range and mapping ¶ The aperture constants changed: ApertureMin / ApertureMax are now 0.7/16 (previously 1/32), and the slider uses a perceptual log-stop mapping so its full travel produces visible depth-of-field change. The aperture property still accepts any positive value; out-of-range values from old presets…

pauseAudio replaced by audioPauseMode ¶ The pauseAudio bool on PhotoModeSettings was replaced by the audioPauseMode enum ( AudioPauseMode.AudioListenerPause / AudioPauseMode.None ). Old serialized assets fall back to the default, AudioListenerPause (the same behavior as the old pauseAudio = true ). No action for most projects. Action required if you had unchecked pauseAudio : select…

Breaking: IPhotoModeEffectsBackend (custom backend implementors only) ¶ If you implemented your own effects backend, two interface changes apply: public interface IPhotoModeEffectsBackend { // New in 1.2.0: report what your backend can do. EffectCapabilities Capabilities { get ; } // Changed in 1.2.0: Initialize() now receives the photo camera. void Initialize ( Camera photoCamera ); //…

Apply the steps above in order (1.2.0 → 1.3.0 → 1.4.0). In practice, unless you wrote a custom effects backend or unchecked pauseAudio , upgrading is: import, reimport, run the Self-Test, and rebuild the demo if you modified it. If anything misbehaves afterwards, start with Troubleshooting .
